description:
Goal: make Compiler2 own a concrete interned type implementation locally, with no production dependency on the old-world `src/types/interned_types` module. Signal: Compiler2 has `compiler2::types::{Types, Ty}` and the full interned-type implementation plus test coverage lives under `src/compiler2/types/`.

Strategy: move the full interned implementation into Compiler2 in total, rename it to the local concrete names Compiler2 actually wants, port both the focused implementation tests and the generic conformance/smoke coverage that currently exercise `InternedConcreteTypes`, and delete the now-unused old-world export surface for that implementation.

Change:
- Add `src/compiler2/types/` by bringing over the full `src/types/interned_types/*` implementation and helper modules.
- Rename `InternedConcreteTypes` to `Types` and `InternedTy` to `Ty` within Compiler2.
- Drop the `crate::types::Types` trait implementation at the Compiler2 boundary; Compiler2 uses inherent methods on its local `Types` directly.
- Port `src/types/interned_types/interned_types_test.rs` into Compiler2.
- Port the larger interned conformance/smoke coverage currently wired through `src/types/mod.rs` so the moved implementation keeps its real safety net.
- Remove the old-world export/test wiring for the unused interned implementation once Compiler2 owns it.

Why:
- Compiler2 should not depend on a pluggable abstraction it does not actually want.
- The interned implementation's ids are only meaningful within one owning store, which makes it a natural Compiler2 world subsystem.
- Pulling it in total, with tests, gives Compiler2 a concrete foundation before production code starts storing local type ids.

Checks:
- All imported type tests pass from their new Compiler2 home.
- No Compiler2 production code imports `crate::types::InternedConcreteTypes` or `src/types/interned_types`.
- Old-world code still builds without the unused interned export surface.
title: Compiler2 types: import the interned type store and its tests in total
